markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define offline/local build expectations from checked-in manifests and package cache assumptions where project policy allows. This MT belongs to NativeDependencyAndPackaging. Make WP-009 native-by-default and fully packaged inside Handshake.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
No proof may require manually launched external services or tools except explicitly allowed model/GGUF/tensor/CUI-portable surfaces.
Bundled frontend/editor libraries must be lockfile-governed, license-reviewed, and callable through Handshake UI/runtime surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: OfflineInstallMode leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: OfflineInstallMode is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: Implemented in wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commit 55da0ff6; proof commands green per delegate report (vitest/cargo/playwright; MT-032 aggregate check:dependency-policy 8/8 pass, app suite 313/313). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Offline proof is genuinely runtime: serves built dist-harness from 127.0.0.1 ONLY and routes page.route('**/*') to ABORT every non-loopback request BEFORE navigation, collecting them into externalRequests. Asserts externalRequests===[] (zero ATTEMPTS, not mere absence of failures).

Typing proof asserts DOCUMENT-MODEL mutation, not just DOM: tiptapDocText() (editor.getText()) must contain the typed paragraph AND the code-block text; code block created via the real ``` StarterKit input rule; Monaco accepts typed input. Worker chunk really fetched from loopback (servedRequests includes worker-*.js).

Caret placement uses the model API (focusFreshLeadingParagraph) but the TYPING itself is real page.keyboard.type - documented reason (headless Ctrl+Home/End lands in taskItem schema). Acceptable; the keystrokes are real.
evasion_probes: Network kill-switch is installed via page.route BEFORE page.goto, so any external fetch during load is aborted+recorded -> a hidden CDN load would populate externalRequests and fail the assertion.
Document-model assertion (getText) defeats a DOM-only fake: text must reach the ProseMirror doc.
evidence_checked: offline_editor_load.spec.ts full read (network abort + ledger + docText + worker-chunk-served assertions).
global_setup builds real harness (vite build, no dev server).

pnpm run check:dependency-policy ran end-to-end with REAL builds (tsc+vite app/dist + build:harness app/dist-harness); exit 0; 8/8 checks pass. worker-bundling proves the static side (0 external refs).

evidence: Implementation commit 55da0ff6 on feat/WP-KERNEL-009; adversarial code review verdict PASS (refutation attempts failed); runtime evidence: check:dependency-policy 8/8 exit 0 (real builds), cargo --lib dependency_policy:: 31/31, vitest 67/67 post-hardening.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
